name: Pairwise Match Delta (Odd vs Even) — Optimized
description: Compute (Player 1 match wins) − (Player 2 match wins) by pairing odd/even numbered games (1 vs 2, 3 vs 4, ...) from `/root/data.xlsx`, using the win metric defined in `/root/background.pdf`, then write the single numeric result to `/root/answer.txt`.
trigger_condition: Use when the task provides `/root/data.xlsx` + `/root/background.pdf` and asks for a single number based on pairing odd games (Player 1) vs even games (Player 2), head-to-head by game number, with output required at `/root/answer.txt` (number only).
usage:
Workflow (minimize I/O and token cost):
1) Extract only minimal PDF text: read `/root/background.pdf` sequentially and stop once you can identify (a) the game-number column name, (b) the comparison metric column name, and (c) win direction (higher/lower wins; ties rule if stated). Do not render pages.
2) Inspect Excel cheaply: open `/root/data.xlsx` and list sheet names; for each sheet read headers only (`nrows=0`) to find the most likely game-level sheet (has a game/index column and the metric column).
3) Load only needed columns from the chosen sheet (`usecols=[game_col, metric_col]`). Coerce `game_col` to integer and `metric_col` to numeric; drop rows where either is missing.
4) Sort by game number, then pair odd/even by number: odd games belong to Player 1; even games to Player 2; pair (1,2),(3,4),…; ignore any unmatched final odd game.
5) Count match wins per pair:
   - Player 1 match win if odd_metric > even_metric (or per PDF win direction).
   - Player 2 match win if even_metric > odd_metric.
   - Ties contribute 0 unless PDF explicitly says otherwise.
6) Compute `delta = (#P1_match_wins) - (#P2_match_wins)` and write ONLY the integer to `/root/answer.txt` (no labels, no newline requirements beyond normal text).
7) Finalization checklist (verifier-critical):
   - Ensure `/root/answer.txt` exists.
   - Re-open and parse it as an integer (strip whitespace).
   - Print the exact file contents at the very end (e.g., `cat /root/answer.txt`) so downstream checks can verify the final artifact text.
